title: Sarcophrynium oxycarpum
intro: Sarcophrynium oxycarpum, also known as the West African yellow ginger, is a plant species belonging to the family Marantaceae, characterized by its bright yellow inflorescences and rhizomatous growth habit. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Sarcophrynium oxycarpum include gingerols, shogaols, and other phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Sarcophrynium oxycarpum is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being dried and used in capsule or powder form to leverage its health benefits.
taxonomy: Sarcophrynium oxycarpum is classified in the family Marantaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the prayer plant family. This family consists of around 31 genera and over 750 species, with many of them native to tropical Africa.
